$schema-note: Build-ready dual-tone token spec for @lms/ui. Maps onto existing --lms-* CSS vars + .lms-* component layer (packages/ui/src/theme.ts + styles.ts). TENANT-SAFE: this file defines ONLY neutrals, semantics, type scale, spacing, radius name->value mappings, shadows, focus ring, and the DEFAULT FALLBACK accent. Per-tenant accent/fontFamily/radius are NEVER defined here — they continue to flow from the tenant brand and override the fallback at runtime.

meta.screen: Foundation — dual-tone design system (@lms/ui token + component layer)
meta.story: #<n> (UI modernization: dual-tone design system — see handshake feat/ui-modernization-dual-tone)
meta.goal: Give @lms/ui a tenant-safe two-tone foundation so admin renders as a data-dense professional B2B dashboard and web renders as an approachable, credible learner/teacher experience, without ever overriding tenant accent/font/radius.
meta.audienceNote: Primary human audience age ~25-55: default body text must be >=16px; admin keeps 16px base but tightens density via spacing/shadow/radius, not by shrinking body text below 16px.
meta.mechanism: tone?: 'admin' | 'web' selected on buildTheme/themeToCssVars/ThemeStyle/AppShell, default 'web' (backward-compatible). tone picks the neutral+semantic set + scale.density below. Tenant accent/font/radius are layered ON TOP and win.

neverDefinedHere: tenant radius choice (sharp/soft/round) — tenant only PICKS a name; the name->value table lives here)

meta.wcag: 2.2 AA minimum for both tones; web body text targets AAA (>=7:1) where feasible.

admin.intent: Cool slate/navy neutrals, crisp and calm. Data-dense B2B SaaS dashboard. Tighter density, small crisp shadows, smaller default radius feel. Min WCAG AA.

contrastChecks.passes: AA large/UI only — do NOT use for body

contrastChecks.note: decorative border; non-text. borderStrong used where 3:1 UI boundary needed.
input outline relies on borderStrong + 1.5px; pairs with focus ring for state. For a strict 3:1 control boundary, focus/invalid states carry the contrast.

density.componentVerticalPaddingHint: tight — buttons/inputs keep 44px min touch target but rows and cards use space-3 vertical rhythm

fallbackAccent.note: Used ONLY when a tenant has not configured an accent. A tenant accent overrides all four. white on #0E5FAE = 5.4:1 (AA).

web.intent: Warmer/softer neutrals (navy + green direction), more whitespace, softer radii, calmer. Learner/teacher: approachable but credible. Body text targets WCAG AAA.

contrastChecks.passes: AAA — body text AAA target met
AA — placeholders/captions only, not long body

contrastChecks.note: decorative; borderStrong for boundaries needing visibility
soft control border; focus/invalid carry state contrast

contrastChecks.passes: AAA — highly visible on light warm surfaces

density.componentVerticalPaddingHint: roomy — cards/sections use space-5/space-6 vertical rhythm; generous whitespace

fallbackAccent.note: Used ONLY when a tenant has not configured an accent. Tenant accent overrides all four. white on #1E4E8C = 8.2:1 (AAA).

scale.note: Shared across both tones except where density (above) overrides fontSize/line/defaultRadiusName/shadowTier. Spacing rhythm and radius name->value mapping are identical so a tenant's radius choice resolves the same numeric value in either app; tone only changes the DEFAULT name when the tenant has not chosen one.

typography.fontFamilyFallback: system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
typography.fontFamilyNote: This is the FALLBACK only. Tenant fontFamily overrides it. Do not hardcode a brand font.
typography.rationale: 16px base chosen for the 25-55 audience and to satisfy web AAA body; admin keeps 16px body and gains density through spacing/shadow/radius, not smaller text.

headingRamp.note: Use clamp() for fluid headings; px values are the upper bound. h1 already fluid in .lms-page-header__title (clamp(22px,5vw,32px)).

space.note: Matches existing --lms-space-1..8. Density is expressed via WHICH step a component reaches for (admin smaller, web larger), not by changing the step values.

radius.note: name->value mapping. Tenant chooses ONE name (sharp/soft/round); tone supplies the DEFAULT name when none chosen (admin=sharp, web=soft). The numeric values per name live here and are tenant-safe.

shadowTiers.note: Two named tiers; tone picks one (admin=crisp, web=soft). Both expose sm/md/lg consumed by --lms-shadow-sm/md and a new --lms-shadow-lg.

motion.reducedMotion: Under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, disable transforms/animations; keep instant state color changes and a static focus ring (extend the existing @media block in styles.ts).
motion.focusRing: 3px solid var(--lms-focus); outline-offset: 2px (keep existing). Visible on ALL interactive elements via :focus-visible.

newCssVars.note: Vars frontend-dev should ADD to themeToCssVars() so component specs below resolve. Existing vars keep their names; these extend the set. Soft semantic backgrounds are emitted as solid tone-specific values (not rgba of tenant accent) so they stay tenant-independent and legible.
newCssVars.migrationNote: Badge/alert rules in styles.ts currently hardcode rgba(15,123,108,...) etc. Replace those with the new --lms-*-soft-bg/--lms-*-soft-text vars so they follow the tone and stay tenant-safe.

components.name: Button
components.purpose: Primary action affordance; primary uses TENANT accent, all other tokens tone-neutral.
components.minTouchTarget: 44x44px (existing min-height:44px kept; --sm is 36px, use only in dense toolbars with adequate spacing)
components.transition: background/color/border-color per scale.motion.hoverTransition; none under reduced-motion

primary.default: bg var(--lms-accent); color var(--lms-accent-contrast); border var(--lms-accent)

secondary.default: bg var(--lms-surface); color var(--lms-text); border 1px solid var(--lms-border-strong)

ghost.default: bg transparent; color var(--lms-text); border transparent

danger.default: bg var(--lms-danger); color var(--lms-danger-contrast); border var(--lms-danger)
danger.hover: bg darken(danger) — frontend may use a slightly darker danger or filter:brightness(0.92)

components.toneNote: secondary border bumped from --lms-border to --lms-border-strong so the control boundary stays perceivable in both tones (border alone is decorative-contrast only).

components.name: Input / Select / Textarea
components.purpose: Text entry; legible boundary + unmistakable focus/invalid states.

states.default: bg var(--lms-surface); color var(--lms-text); border 1px solid var(--lms-border-strong); radius var(--lms-radius-sm)
states.placeholder: color var(--lms-text-subtle) — captions-grade contrast, never used for entered values
states.focus-visible: outline 3px solid var(--lms-focus); outline-offset 2px; border-color var(--lms-focus)
states.invalid: border-color var(--lms-danger); aria-invalid=true; field error text color var(--lms-danger) with leading inline-SVG warning icon (not emoji)

components.labelSpec: label var(--lms-text) weight 600 size 14px; help var(--lms-text-muted) 13px; error var(--lms-danger) 13px
components.a11y: Every control has an associated <label>; error referenced via aria-describedby; invalid via aria-invalid.

components.name: Card / Surface
components.purpose: Content container; interactive variant is a clickable surface (links to detail).

states.static: bg var(--lms-surface); border 1px solid var(--lms-border); radius var(--lms-radius-md); shadow var(--lms-shadow-sm); padding admin space-4 / web space-5
states.interactive-hover: shadow var(--lms-shadow-md); admin translateY(-1px) / web translateY(-2px); under reduced-motion no transform

components.toneNote: admin cards = crisp shadow + sharp radius + tighter padding; web cards = soft shadow + soft radius + roomier padding. Pure token-driven, no forked CSS.

components.name: Table / DataTable row
components.selector: .lms-table, .lms-table__row (NEW — frontend adds; admin-primary but available to both)
components.purpose: Data-dense listing (admin users/reports). Scannable rows with hover + optional zebra.

states.header: bg var(--lms-surface-2); color var(--lms-text-muted); font-size 14px; weight 600; text-transform none; border-bottom 1px solid var(--lms-border-strong)
states.row-default: bg var(--lms-surface); border-bottom 1px solid var(--lms-border); td padding admin space-2 space-3 / web space-3 space-4
states.row-zebra: optional: even rows bg var(--lms-surface-2) at low emphasis — admin default ON, web default OFF
states.row-hover: bg var(--lms-surface-2-hover); transition background per motion; cursor pointer when row is a link
states.sort-active-th: color var(--lms-text); inline-SVG caret indicates asc/desc

responsive.phone: stack to card-list (label:value pairs) OR horizontal scroll container with role=region aria-label + visible affordance; never silent overflow

components.a11y: Native <table> with <th scope>; sort controls are <button> in <th> with aria-sort; row links wrap the primary cell, not the whole row, for screen-reader clarity.

components.name: Badge

tones.accent: bg var(--lms-accent-soft); color var(--lms-accent) — tenant accent (only accent-derived token in this list)

components.note: All soft pairs verified >=6:1 (see contrastChecks). Replaces today's hardcoded rgba badge colors with tone-aware vars.

components.name: Alert
components.purpose: Inline contextual message; the natural home for permission-denied and error flows.

tones.info: bg var(--lms-info-soft-bg); border 1px solid (info @ ~25% or --lms-border-strong); body var(--lms-text)

anatomy: leading inline-SVG icon (per tone, NOT emoji)
components.a11y: role=status (info/success) or role=alert (warning/danger); icon aria-hidden, meaning carried by text.

components.name: Nav / Topbar
components.purpose: App shell chrome; persistent brand + primary nav + actions.

states.topbar: bg var(--lms-surface); border-bottom 1px solid var(--lms-border); admin min-height 56px tight / web min-height 60px
states.nav-link-default: color var(--lms-text-muted); padding space-2 space-3; radius var(--lms-radius-sm)

components.toneNote: admin shell may carry a left sidebar nav for data-dense IA; web shell stays top-nav + roomy main. Same tokens, layout differs per app composition (not forked CSS in @lms/ui).
components.responsive: topbar wraps gracefully; on phone collapse nav into a disclosure (<button aria-expanded>) — no horizontal overflow at 360px.
components.a11y: <header> landmark + <nav aria-label>; main is <main>; active link aria-current=page.

components.name: EmptyState
components.purpose: Communicate 'nothing here yet' with a clear next action; replaces emoji icons (👤/📖).
components.iconSpec: render chosen emptyStateIcons SVG sized ~40-48px, stroke=var(--lms-text-subtle), aria-hidden=true; remove .lms-empty-state__icon font-size emoji styling

users.title: No users yet
users.desc: Invite teammates to this organization to manage their access and roles.

courses.title: No courses yet
courses.desc: Create your first course to start building lessons and enrolling learners.

content.title: No content here
content.desc: Add lessons, files, or assignments to fill out this section.

searchNone.title: No results found
searchNone.desc: Try a different search term or clear your filters.

generic.title: Nothing to show
generic.desc: There's nothing here yet. Check back later or take an action to get started.

emptyStateIcons.note: Inline SVG path data, Heroicons/Lucide-style outline. Render in a 24x24 viewBox, fill='none', stroke='currentColor' (currentColor = --lms-text-subtle via inline style), stroke-width=1.6, stroke-linecap='round', stroke-linejoin='round', aria-hidden='true'. Scale up via width/height ~40-48px. NO emoji.

semanticInlineIcons.note: For Alert/Field error icons — same render rules.

flows: Happy path — admin data-dense table renders with hover/zebra/sort; web cards render roomy with soft shadows; tenant accent applied on primary buttons/links only.
Empty state — inline-SVG EmptyState with real copy and a primary action (no emoji).
Error — Alert --danger with inline-SVG icon + retry action.
Permission denied (non-admin) — Alert --warning/--danger: 'You don't have access to this area. Contact your organization admin if you think this is a mistake.' inside a Card; no data leak.
Disabled — buttons/inputs opacity-reduced, pointer-events none, aria-disabled.

accessibility.contrastMin: 4.5:1 body text / 3:1 large text & UI components & focus ring
accessibility.focusOrder: logical DOM order; visible :focus-visible ring (3px solid var(--lms-focus), offset 2px) on every interactive element in BOTH tones
accessibility.focusRingContrast: admin focus #1D5FD6 >=3.9:1 on all surfaces; web focus #1E4E8C >=7:1 on all surfaces
accessibility.motion: respect prefers-reduced-motion (extend existing @media block); transitions 150-300ms otherwise
accessibility.touchTargets: 44x44px min for all controls (existing min-height kept)
accessibility.semantics: header/nav/main/footer landmarks; h1->h6 ramp; native controls labelled; aria only where native won't do; icons aria-hidden with text equivalents
accessibility.noColorOnly: status never conveyed by color alone — badges/alerts carry text + inline-SVG icon

openQuestions: Default tone per app: admin app should pass tone='admin', web app tone='web'. Confirm AppShell default stays 'web' for backward compatibility (per orchestrator decision) — frontend to thread tone at each app's AppShell call site.
Admin IA: does admin want a persistent left sidebar nav (recommended for data density) or stay top-nav? Affects shell composition only, not @lms/ui tokens. Needs product confirmation.
Table component is net-new in @lms/ui (today screens likely roll their own). Confirm we standardize .lms-table here vs leaving tables app-local. Recommended: add it to @lms/ui for consistency.
Dark mode: spec'd as stretch only (handshake). If pursued, both tones need a parallel dark neutral+semantic set behind prefers-color-scheme — out of scope for AC sign-off.
Zebra striping default: proposed admin ON / web OFF — confirm with product, easily toggled via a modifier class.
